title: Bothriocline ruwenzoriensis
taxonomy: Bothriocline ruwenzoriensis is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Bothriocline, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical Africa. Bothriocline ruwenzoriensis is further classified within the tribe Inuleae, a subfamily of daisy-like plants. The species is endemic to the high-altitude mountain regions of East Africa, particularly in the Ruwenzori Mountains. Bothriocline ruwenzoriensis is often found growing among rocky outcrops and grasslands.
uses: Bothriocline ruwenzoriensis is a plant species used in traditional medicine in various parts of Africa. Its medicinal properties have been utilized to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues. The plant has been used to make remedies for respiratory problems and to boost the immune system. In some cultures, it is also used to treat wounds and skin conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal applications and their effectiveness.
